facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Old service court west of South Third, at a patched masonry wall and chained abandoned door that likely conceal an alternate route linked to the sealed bell-foundation structure beneath the cracked street

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Unranked, unlicensed salvage runner; shoulder still in sling and increasingly unreliable, headache and metallic aftertaste worsened by repeated listening strain; provisional notice and pending deposition remain active; now under direct Review scrutiny after carefully withholding route details, but has privately identified a probable hidden access point with interior airflow and matching broken bell-resonance

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Use Pell’s short window before first bell to verify or exploit the newly found maybe-door/hidden stair route before Review, Bren, or Pike’s people seize or bury it, while preserving the truth about Benn, Derren, and the sealed structure

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Rowan still has no lawful authority, rank, sponsor, or money cushion; Maelin’s night hold remains in force at South Third; Pell will file a supplemental report by first bell; Hadrik now specifically suspects omitted egress information; Rowan is injured, time-starved, and cannot breach the hidden wall quietly or quickly with one arm

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Lio remains Rowan’s active field ally and goes with him to search; Pell becomes a stronger but conditional procedural shield who grants time until first bell and will phrase any usable disclosure narrowly; Mara Arl accepts a surveillance role on Bren’s orbit and remains committed to untrimmed truth about Benn; Maelin still functions as a hard district check against contractor seizure; Nessa remains an off-site archival ally whose earlier trace underpins the bell-foundation interpretation

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: The struggle shifts from controlling the sealed street scene to racing over a newly located probable alternate entrance: Review, district, and Pike-aligned interests all contest who gets to define access and evidence, while Rowan must decide whether caution protects lives or costs them the remaining hours if survivors are still below
